problem: My grandpa has 10 pieces of art, including 3 prints by Escher. If he hangs the pieces of art in a row in a random order, what is the probability that all three pieces by Escher will be placed consecutively?

classified_questions.question: How many total pieces of art does your grandpa have?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many prints by Escher does your grandpa have?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If all the pieces of art are hung in a row randomly, what is the total number of possible arrangements?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many ways can the three prints by Escher be placed consecutively?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why can we treat the three Escher prints as one item when counting the arrangements?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many locations are there for this "combined" item in the row of art?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of ways to place the three Escher prints without any restrictions?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the probability of the three Escher prints being placed consecutively?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final probability of the three Escher prints being placed consecutively?
classified_questions.label: b
